idiom: cost an arm and a leg
example: Repairing the car cost an arm and a leg.

meaning: чувствовать себя немного больным или уставшим
example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.
We go out for dinner once in a blue moon.

meaning: рассказать секрет, который нужно было хранить
example: She spilled the beans about the surprise party.

idiom: let the cat out of the bag
example: I let the cat out of the bag about the promotion.
He'll clean his room when pigs fly.
We burned the midnight oil to finish the project.
The party got out of hand quickly.

translation: сделать больше, чем от тебя ждут
example: Our team always goes the extra mile for clients.

meaning: наблюдать или заботиться о ком-то или чём-то
example: Can you keep an eye on my bag?
I'm just pulling your leg — don't worry.
You can't sit on the fence forever. Choose!
That name rings a bell, but I'm not sure.
We don't always see eye to eye, but we respect each other.
I don't have a plan — let's play it by ear.
I'm exhausted — time to hit the sack.

meaning: ошибаться в поиске причины или человека
example: If you think I took your pen, you're barking up the wrong tree.

idiom: get a taste of your own medicine
meaning: столкнуться с тем же плохим обращением, которое ты показывал другим
example: She's always rude — now she got a taste of her own medicine.
It's late — I should hit the road.
I'm keeping my fingers crossed for your interview.

meaning: привлечь внимание к себе, когда кто-то другой этого заслуживает
example: She announced her engagement at my birthday party — she stole my thunder.
Our vacation plans are still up in the air.
He's buttering up the boss for a promotion.

meaning: делать что-то плохо, чтобы сэкономить время или деньги
example: Don't cut corners — do it properly.

meaning: получить информацию от самого надёжного источника
example: I heard it straight from the horse's mouth — the manager told me.
